meta.disclaimer: Generated from public market data and news for research and education. Not financial advice; data may be delayed, incomplete, or wrong.

items.title: Advancing Mathematics Research with AI-Driven Formal Proof Search
items.abstract: This paper introduces and evaluates a method where Large Language Models (LLMs) generate formal proofs in languages like Lean to overcome their inherent unreliability in mathematical reasoning. The core contribution is the first large-scale demonstration of this AI-driven formal proof search, showing agents autonomously solved 9 open Erdős problems and proved 44 OEIS conjectures, validating the approach for active mathematical research.
items.figure_caption: Example inputs/outputs for an AlphaProof-equipped agent (applied to Erdős #125). The user provides a Lean file with a specification of the problem, and an empty proof body replaced with the sorry placeholder. (a) Modifications are permitted only within EVOLVE-BLOCK and EVOLVE-VALUE markers. (b) During sketch refinement, the prover subagent is shown an assembled prompt template with the current proof, and optionally prior attempts/sketches, their Elo ratings, and feedback from AlphaProof’s attempts on unsolved goals. (c) The prover reasons about the problem informally and invokes tools. In this example, the prover invoked AlphaProof which resolved all but one goal. The prover then decomposed that goal into three simpler lemmas, and called AlphaProof again, which then resolved all remaining goals. The agent also produced a natural language summary of its attempt at the end of generation.

items.title: Agentic CLEAR: Automating Multi-Level Evaluation of LLM Agents
items.abstract: Agentic CLEAR is an automatic, dynamic evaluation framework designed to address the challenges of assessing complex LLM agent behavior. It provides multi-level textual insights into agent actions at the system, trace, and node levels, moving beyond basic observability tools. The framework's core contribution is offering high-quality, data-driven feedback that aligns well with human judgment, making agent evaluation more accessible and adaptable.
items.figure_caption: Agentic CLEAR Pipeline. We start by preparing the execution traces. Stage 1: Apply multi-level per-trace evaluation via an LLM Judge. Stage 2: Aggregate insights using CLEAR, split into System-wide patterns and Node-specific patterns, and prepare them for the UI.

items.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
items.abstract: This paper introduces the "Accumulated Message Effect on LLM Judgments" (AMEL), demonstrating that the polarity of prior conversation history biases subsequent evaluations made by Large Language Models. Across numerous tests, models shifted their judgments toward the prevailing sentiment of the preceding messages, particularly when the item being judged was inherently uncertain. Crucially, this bias was found to be independent of the length of the preceding context.
items.figure_caption: Overview of AMEL. (a) Items where the model is uncertain at baseline absorb the most bias ( d = − 0.34 d=-0.34 ); confident-baseline items absorb less ( d = − 0.15 d=-0.15 ). (b) Negative context biases models more than positive context (paired per-item ratio 1.62 × 1.62\( \times \) , p < 10 − 39 p<10^{-39} ); marginal means yield ≈ 2 × \( \approx \) 2\( \times \) (Section 4.5 ). Even balanced history shifts models toward “no.” (c) Bias saturates immediately; 5 turns produce the same effect as 50.

items.title: Can AI Make Conflicts Worse? An Alignment Failure in LLM Deployment Across Conflict Contexts
items.abstract: This paper investigates the risk of Large Language Models (LLMs) exacerbating armed conflicts by generating harmful outputs like false equivalencies or genocide denial. The authors tested nine model configurations across 90 multi-turn conflict scenarios, finding failure rates ranging from 6% to 47%. The core contribution is demonstrating that model choice is a significant safety concern in conflict contexts, as misaligned outputs can deepen societal divisions.
items.figure_caption: Mean conflict-insensitivity score (bars, left axis) and failure rate (line, right axis) by model. Based on 90 conversations per model.

items.title: Claw AI Lab: An Autonomous Multi-Agent Research Team
items.abstract: Claw AI Lab introduces an autonomous research platform that moves beyond single-agent pipelines by enabling users to instantiate and manage a customizable, multi-agent research team from a single prompt. Its core contribution is providing an interactive, laboratory-like environment with real-time monitoring, collaborative workflows, and granular control (rollback/resume). This is facilitated by the Claw-Code Harness, which tightly integrates local codebases and execution artifacts back into the autonomous research loop, significantly improving experimental completion.
items.figure_caption: Overview of Claw AI Lab. The system organizes automatic research into five connected layers: idea, planning, coding, experimentation, and writing layers. Each layer uses specialized agents and validation loops, while feedback can flow across layers to revise earlier decisions when needed.

items.title: Contractual Skills: A GovernSpec Design Framework for Enterprise AI Agents
items.abstract: This paper introduces **Contractual Skills**, a design framework inspired by GovernSpec, to structure agent skills as inspectable, readable task contracts within enterprise AI systems. The core method organizes `SKILL.md` files to explicitly define goals, boundaries, contracts, and verification steps, clarifying the boundaries between skills and formal governance/runtime systems. This contributes a standardized way to embed governance requirements directly into lightweight skill definitions for better enterprise oversight.
items.figure_caption: Contractual skills sit between a structured task contract and runtime enforcement. They make task intent and boundaries inspectable, while tool adapters and guardrails remain responsible for enforcement.

items.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
items.abstract: DeltaBox addresses the bottleneck of slow state checkpoint/rollback (C/R) for stateful AI agents by proposing a change-based transactional C/R mechanism instead of full state duplication. The core method introduces **DeltaState**, a new OS-level abstraction featuring **DeltaFS** (layered filesystem C/R) and a mechanism for tracking memory/process changes. This significantly reduces C/R latency to millisecond levels, enabling faster state exploration for agents.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Pass rate on SWE-bench Verified. (a) Linear ReAct vs. MCTS across three coding models. (b) Base vs. RL-trained across three open-weight model families.

items.title: Post-Training is About States, Not Tokens: A State Distribution View of SFT, RL, and On-Policy Distillation
items.abstract: This paper reframes post-training methods like SFT and RL not just by their loss functions, but by how they shape the **state distribution** used for learning. The core contribution is formalizing post-training as **state-distribution shaping**, demonstrating that the states induced by the learner (as in RL/OPD) versus fixed dataset states (as in SFT) critically impact performance and retention.

items.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
items.abstract: This paper addresses covert political bias in LLMs, where models handle opposing political topics asymmetrically. The authors introduce two metrics, Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency, to quantify this bias. They propose Political Consistency Training (PCT), an RL method combining these two consistency paradigms, to substantially reduce this bias while maintaining overall model helpfulness.

items.title: Spreadsheet-RL: Advancing Large Language Model Agents on Realistic Spreadsheet Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
items.abstract: Spreadsheet-RL is a reinforcement learning fine-tuning framework designed to train specialized AI agents for complex, multi-step tasks within a realistic Microsoft Excel environment. The core method involves using RL to overcome the limitations of simple prompting methods for real-world spreadsheet workflows. Its contribution is a specialized framework and a collection of domain-specific evaluation tasks to advance LLM agents in practical spreadsheet automation.

items.title: Think Thrice Before You Speak: Dual knowledge-enhanced Theory-of-Mind Reasoning for Persuasive Agents
items.abstract: This paper introduces **TTBYS (Think Thrice Before You Speak)**, a novel framework that enhances Large Language Models' (LLMs) Theory of Mind (ToM) reasoning for persuasive dialogue. TTBYS uses a **dual knowledge enhancement** approach within a stepwise reasoning process to explicitly model the sequential dependencies among mental states (Belief, Desire, Intention). The core contribution is providing a robust method and the **ToM-BPD dataset** to overcome fragmented mental state representations in persuasive agent design.
items.figure_caption: Illustration of self BDI state evolution and BDI-based inference for ToM-driven persuasive dialogue (ToM-PD). The left panel shows the internal reasoning process, where Lucian generates actions through the evolution of its belief, desire, and intention states based on self-perception and experience. The middle panel presents a multi-turn persuasive dialogue scenario between the agent and the user. The right panel depicts the ToM-PD process, where the agent observes user actions (utterances), infers the user’s latent BDI states, and dynamically selects appropriate persuasive strategies to guide subsequent actions.

items.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
items.abstract: This paper investigates how data ordering during pre-training affects the temporal knowledge of Large Language Models (LLMs). The authors introduce a benchmark of over 7,000 temporally grounded questions to assess time-sensitive factual recall. They demonstrate that training LLMs on chronologically ordered data, rather than shuffled data, results in models with more up-to-date and temporally precise knowledge without sacrificing general language understanding.
items.figure_caption: Yearly temporal knowledge with Kairos. Relative gains in F1 score on KairosQA between the 2020–2021 and 2023–2024 periods for our sequentially pre-trained model versus other open-source base models (ordered by their release date with the most recent at the right). These results highlight that even for recently released open-source base models, shuffled pre-training leads to a temporal delay in knowledge; performance decays when querying recent facts, even those preceding the training cut-off. Conversely, sequential pre-training represents a significant step toward developing more up-to-date models.

items.title: WorkstreamBench: Evaluating LLM Agents on End-to-End Spreadsheet Tasks in Finance
items.abstract: This paper introduces **WorkstreamBench**, a novel benchmark designed to evaluate Large Language Model (LLM) agents on complex, end-to-end spreadsheet creation tasks relevant to finance, such as financial modeling. The core contribution is moving beyond simple formula edits to assess agents' ability to produce complete, economically critical artifacts. Evaluation incorporates multidimensional criteria beyond simple correctness, focusing on aspects like readability crucial for multi-stakeholder review.
items.figure_caption: Compared to prior work that focus on atomic tasks on spreadsheet ( left ), WorkstreamBench evaluates LLM agents on completing end-to-end spreadsheet tasks in critical finance domain ( right ), covering key criteria that determines usability of resulting deliverable in professional settings. Prior tasks focus on simple atomic tasks that center on question-answering or edits involving few values or formula, where evaluation can largely be performed via exact-matching. In contrast, WorkstreamBench expects a complete multi-sheet workbook, and consequently employs a holistic evaluation centered on high-level quality relevant in professional settings (e.g. readability).

items.title: GraphFlow: A Graph-Based Workflow Management for Efficient LLM-Agent Serving
items.abstract: GraphFlow introduces a novel graph-based workflow management system for efficient LLM-agent serving. It represents workflows as a unified graph structure, wGraph, allowing for dynamic instantiation of task-specific workflows based on semantic understanding. This approach overcomes the limitations of static templates by enabling adaptive workflow generation that better captures deep relationships for generalized task execution.
items.figure_caption: Structured agentic workflow for complex online shopping. The agent executes a set of atomic operations (e.g., search, filter, review) to fulfill the user query.

items.title: Boiling the Frog: A Multi-Turn Benchmark for Agentic Safety
items.abstract: This paper introduces "Boiling the Frog," a novel benchmark designed to evaluate the safety of tool-using AI agents in office environments against **incremental attacks**. The core method involves multi-turn scenarios where benign initial requests gradually escalate to risk-bearing actions within a persistent workspace. Its contribution is shifting safety evaluation from static text outputs to dynamic, stateful agent behavior susceptible to gradual manipulation.
items.figure_caption: Boiling the Frog four-stage pipeline. Starting from regulatory and BF agentic risk categories (Stage 0), each scenario is instantiated in a sandboxed Docker workspace (Stage 1), planned as a multi-turn chain with escalating risk (Stage 2), executed as an agent trajectory (Stage 3), and validated through artifact-based scoring (Stage 4).

items.title: LANG: Reinforcement Learning for Multilingual Reasoning with Language-Adaptive Hint Guidance
items.abstract: LANG is a novel reinforcement learning framework designed to improve multilingual reasoning in LLMs by using language-conditioned hints to guide exploration in non-English tasks. It prevents over-reliance on these hints through a progressive decay schedule and a language-adaptive switch tailored to specific language difficulties. This approach substantially enhances reasoning performance across challenging multilingual benchmarks while maintaining input language consistency.

items.title: AtelierEval: Agentic Evaluation of Humans & LLMs as Text-to-Image Prompters
items.abstract: AtelierEval is introduced as the first unified benchmark to quantify the prompting proficiency of both humans and MLLMs in generating text-to-image prompts across 360 expert-crafted tasks. The core method involves using AtelierJudge, a skill-based, memory-augmented agentic evaluator, to produce reliable subjective and objective scores for prompt-image pairs. This contribution enables the systematic evaluation of the crucial upstream prompting component, which was previously unmeasured in T2I benchmarks.
items.figure_caption: MLLMs act as prompters in diverse T2I workflows, translating user intent into effective prompts.

items.title: Beyond Acoustic Emotion Recognition: Multimodal Pathos Analysis in Political Speech Using LLM-Based and Acoustic Emotion Models
items.abstract: This paper compares acoustic emotion models and LLMs for analyzing the Pathos dimension in political speech, using the TRUST LLM pipeline as a benchmark. The core finding is that the Gemini LLM, analyzing both audio and transcript, correlates strongly with the benchmark Pathos scores, while a standard acoustic SER model does not. This suggests LLMs are more effective proxies for complex emotional dimensions like Pathos than purely acoustic features alone.

items.title: Beyond Temperature: Hyperfitting as a Late-Stage Geometric Expansion
items.abstract: This paper investigates "Hyperfitting," a phenomenon where extreme fine-tuning enhances LLM generation quality beyond simple distribution sharpening. The authors demonstrate that hyperfitting is fundamentally distinct from temperature scaling, as entropy-matched controls fail to replicate its diversity gains. Their core contribution is identifying that hyperfitting relies on a dynamic, context-dependent rank reordering mechanism localized to a "Terminal Expansion" in the final transformer block.
items.figure_caption: The Rank Reordering Mechanism Enabling Late-Stage Efficiency. (A) Temperature scaling (T < < 1.0) sharpens the probability distribution but preserves the original ranking, leaving the repetitive token (Token A) as the winner. (B) Hyperfitting fundamentally alters the output distribution by reordering ranks — suppressing repetitive candidates and promoting diverse, context-dependent candidates (Token B) to the Top-1 position. This bidirectional effect distinguishes hyperfitting from simple temperature scaling and reveals that the generative capability is localized, motivating our parameter-efficient Late-Stage LoRA strategy.

items.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
items.abstract: LCGuard is a framework designed to ensure safe latent communication via shared Key-Value (KV) caches in multi-agent LLM systems. It addresses the risk of sensitive information leakage by learning representation-level transformations on the KV caches before they are transmitted between agents. This acts as a "guard" to control the flow of potentially sensitive intermediate reasoning states encoded in the latent space.
items.figure_caption: Multi-agent communication topologies: sequential, hierarchical, and graph-based. Edges carry KV cache latent artifacts m i ​ j m_{ij} .

items.title: AI is just unauthorised plagiarism at a bigger scale
items.summary: The article argues that current AI models fundamentally operate as large-scale, unauthorized plagiarism by remixing vast amounts of existing, uncredited training data.

items.title: Shunning AI is the human choice
items.summary: The article argues that actively choosing to reject or limit the use of artificial intelligence is a valid and beneficial human decision.

items.title: BBEdit 16
items.summary: BBEdit 16 has been released, featuring significant updates including a completely redesigned interface, enhanced performance, and new features for text and code editing.

items.title: News outlets are limiting the Internet Archive’s access to their journalism
items.summary: Over 340 local news outlets are restricting the Internet Archive's ability to access and preserve their published journalism.

items.title: The memory shortage is causing a repricing of consumer electronics
items.summary: The ongoing memory shortage, driven partly by AI demands, is forcing a significant price increase across various consumer electronics, including smartphones.

items.title: Samsung chip workers will get an average $340k bonus as AI profits soar
items.summary: Samsung is awarding its chip division employees an average bonus of $340,000 due to soaring profits driven by the high demand for AI-related semiconductors.

items.title: Steve Wozniak cheered after telling students they have AI – actual intelligence
items.summary: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak encouraged students in a graduation speech, emphasizing that they possess genuine intelligence distinct from artificial intelligence.

items.title: Multi-Stream LLMs: new paper on parallelizing/separating prompts, thinking, I/O
items.summary: A new research paper introduces Multi-Stream LLMs, a technique for improving efficiency by parallelizing and separating the processing of prompts, internal reasoning, and input/output operations.

items.title: Tell HN: I'm tired of AI-generated answers
items.summary: A user on Hacker News expressed frustration with the increasing prevalence and perceived low quality of AI-generated answers across various platforms.

items.description: Official, Anthropic-managed directory of high quality Claude Code Plugins.
items.summary: The official Anthropic repository provides a curated directory of high-quality, managed Claude Code Plugins for extending Claude's capabilities.

items.description: Pre-indexed code knowledge graph for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and OpenCode — fewer tokens, fewer tool calls, 100% local
items.summary: CodeGraph is a pre-indexed, local knowledge graph designed to enhance large language models like Claude Code and Codex by providing relevant code context with fewer tokens and tool calls.

items.description: π RuView turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time spatial intelligence, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video.
items.summary: RuView transforms commodity WiFi signals into real-time spatial intelligence, enabling vital sign monitoring and presence detection without using any video.

items.description: Learn it. Build it. Ship it for others.
items.summary: This repository provides a comprehensive, hands-on guide to learning, building, and deploying AI engineering projects from the ground up.
The Chrome DevTools MCP repository provides tools and infrastructure to enable coding agents to interact with and debug web applications using the Chrome DevTools Protocol.

items.description: Repository for skills to assist AI coding agents with .NET and C#
items.summary: The dotnet/skills repository provides essential skills and knowledge for AI coding agents to effectively assist with .NET and C# development tasks.

items.description: Graphs that teach > graphs that impress. Turn any code into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about. Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, and more.
items.summary: This repository transforms code into an interactive knowledge graph for exploration, searching, and querying, supporting various AI coding assistants like Claude Code and Copilot.

items.description: Odoo. Open Source Apps To Grow Your Business.
items.summary: Odoo is an open-source suite of integrated business applications designed to manage and grow various aspects of a company, offering modules for CRM, e-commerce, accounting, and more.
This repository provides a command-line interface (CLI) tool, `cfnew`, to quickly create new Cloudflare Workers projects with boilerplate code and configuration.

arxiv.title: Post-Training is About States, Not Tokens: A State Distribution View of SFT, RL, and On-Policy Distillation
arxiv.abstract: This paper reframes post-training methods like SFT and RL not just by their loss functions, but by how they shape the **state distribution** used for learning. The core contribution is formalizing post-training as **state-distribution shaping**, demonstrating that the states induced by the learner (as in RL/OPD) versus fixed dataset states (as in SFT) critically impact performance and retention.

arxiv.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
arxiv.abstract: This paper addresses covert political bias in LLMs, where models handle opposing political topics asymmetrically. The authors introduce two metrics, Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency, to quantify this bias. They propose Political Consistency Training (PCT), an RL method combining these two consistency paradigms, to substantially reduce this bias while maintaining overall model helpfulness.

arxiv.title: Spreadsheet-RL: Advancing Large Language Model Agents on Realistic Spreadsheet Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
arxiv.abstract: Spreadsheet-RL is a reinforcement learning fine-tuning framework designed to train specialized AI agents for complex, multi-step tasks within a realistic Microsoft Excel environment. The core method involves using RL to overcome the limitations of simple prompting methods for real-world spreadsheet workflows. Its contribution is a specialized framework and a collection of domain-specific evaluation tasks to advance LLM agents in practical spreadsheet automation.

arxiv.title: Think Thrice Before You Speak: Dual knowledge-enhanced Theory-of-Mind Reasoning for Persuasive Agents
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **TTBYS (Think Thrice Before You Speak)**, a novel framework that enhances Large Language Models' (LLMs) Theory of Mind (ToM) reasoning for persuasive dialogue. TTBYS uses a **dual knowledge enhancement** approach within a stepwise reasoning process to explicitly model the sequential dependencies among mental states (Belief, Desire, Intention). The core contribution is providing a robust method and the **ToM-BPD dataset** to overcome fragmented mental state representations in persuasive agent design.
arxiv.figure_caption: Illustration of self BDI state evolution and BDI-based inference for ToM-driven persuasive dialogue (ToM-PD). The left panel shows the internal reasoning process, where Lucian generates actions through the evolution of its belief, desire, and intention states based on self-perception and experience. The middle panel presents a multi-turn persuasive dialogue scenario between the agent and the user. The right panel depicts the ToM-PD process, where the agent observes user actions (utterances), infers the user’s latent BDI states, and dynamically selects appropriate persuasive strategies to guide subsequent actions.

arxiv.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
arxiv.abstract: This paper investigates how data ordering during pre-training affects the temporal knowledge of Large Language Models (LLMs). The authors introduce a benchmark of over 7,000 temporally grounded questions to assess time-sensitive factual recall. They demonstrate that training LLMs on chronologically ordered data, rather than shuffled data, results in models with more up-to-date and temporally precise knowledge without sacrificing general language understanding.
arxiv.figure_caption: Yearly temporal knowledge with Kairos. Relative gains in F1 score on KairosQA between the 2020–2021 and 2023–2024 periods for our sequentially pre-trained model versus other open-source base models (ordered by their release date with the most recent at the right). These results highlight that even for recently released open-source base models, shuffled pre-training leads to a temporal delay in knowledge; performance decays when querying recent facts, even those preceding the training cut-off. Conversely, sequential pre-training represents a significant step toward developing more up-to-date models.

arxiv.title: WorkstreamBench: Evaluating LLM Agents on End-to-End Spreadsheet Tasks in Finance
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **WorkstreamBench**, a novel benchmark designed to evaluate Large Language Model (LLM) agents on complex, end-to-end spreadsheet creation tasks relevant to finance, such as financial modeling. The core contribution is moving beyond simple formula edits to assess agents' ability to produce complete, economically critical artifacts. Evaluation incorporates multidimensional criteria beyond simple correctness, focusing on aspects like readability crucial for multi-stakeholder review.
arxiv.figure_caption: Compared to prior work that focus on atomic tasks on spreadsheet ( left ), WorkstreamBench evaluates LLM agents on completing end-to-end spreadsheet tasks in critical finance domain ( right ), covering key criteria that determines usability of resulting deliverable in professional settings. Prior tasks focus on simple atomic tasks that center on question-answering or edits involving few values or formula, where evaluation can largely be performed via exact-matching. In contrast, WorkstreamBench expects a complete multi-sheet workbook, and consequently employs a holistic evaluation centered on high-level quality relevant in professional settings (e.g. readability).

arxiv.title: GraphFlow: A Graph-Based Workflow Management for Efficient LLM-Agent Serving
arxiv.abstract: GraphFlow introduces a novel graph-based workflow management system for efficient LLM-agent serving. It represents workflows as a unified graph structure, wGraph, allowing for dynamic instantiation of task-specific workflows based on semantic understanding. This approach overcomes the limitations of static templates by enabling adaptive workflow generation that better captures deep relationships for generalized task execution.
arxiv.figure_caption: Structured agentic workflow for complex online shopping. The agent executes a set of atomic operations (e.g., search, filter, review) to fulfill the user query.

arxiv.title: Boiling the Frog: A Multi-Turn Benchmark for Agentic Safety
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces "Boiling the Frog," a novel benchmark designed to evaluate the safety of tool-using AI agents in office environments against **incremental attacks**. The core method involves multi-turn scenarios where benign initial requests gradually escalate to risk-bearing actions within a persistent workspace. Its contribution is shifting safety evaluation from static text outputs to dynamic, stateful agent behavior susceptible to gradual manipulation.
arxiv.figure_caption: Boiling the Frog four-stage pipeline. Starting from regulatory and BF agentic risk categories (Stage 0), each scenario is instantiated in a sandboxed Docker workspace (Stage 1), planned as a multi-turn chain with escalating risk (Stage 2), executed as an agent trajectory (Stage 3), and validated through artifact-based scoring (Stage 4).

arxiv.title: LANG: Reinforcement Learning for Multilingual Reasoning with Language-Adaptive Hint Guidance
arxiv.abstract: LANG is a novel reinforcement learning framework designed to improve multilingual reasoning in LLMs by using language-conditioned hints to guide exploration in non-English tasks. It prevents over-reliance on these hints through a progressive decay schedule and a language-adaptive switch tailored to specific language difficulties. This approach substantially enhances reasoning performance across challenging multilingual benchmarks while maintaining input language consistency.

arxiv.title: AtelierEval: Agentic Evaluation of Humans & LLMs as Text-to-Image Prompters
arxiv.abstract: AtelierEval is introduced as the first unified benchmark to quantify the prompting proficiency of both humans and MLLMs in generating text-to-image prompts across 360 expert-crafted tasks. The core method involves using AtelierJudge, a skill-based, memory-augmented agentic evaluator, to produce reliable subjective and objective scores for prompt-image pairs. This contribution enables the systematic evaluation of the crucial upstream prompting component, which was previously unmeasured in T2I benchmarks.
arxiv.figure_caption: MLLMs act as prompters in diverse T2I workflows, translating user intent into effective prompts.

arxiv.title: Beyond Acoustic Emotion Recognition: Multimodal Pathos Analysis in Political Speech Using LLM-Based and Acoustic Emotion Models
arxiv.abstract: This paper compares acoustic emotion models and LLMs for analyzing the Pathos dimension in political speech, using the TRUST LLM pipeline as a benchmark. The core finding is that the Gemini LLM, analyzing both audio and transcript, correlates strongly with the benchmark Pathos scores, while a standard acoustic SER model does not. This suggests LLMs are more effective proxies for complex emotional dimensions like Pathos than purely acoustic features alone.

arxiv.title: Beyond Temperature: Hyperfitting as a Late-Stage Geometric Expansion
arxiv.abstract: This paper investigates "Hyperfitting," a phenomenon where extreme fine-tuning enhances LLM generation quality beyond simple distribution sharpening. The authors demonstrate that hyperfitting is fundamentally distinct from temperature scaling, as entropy-matched controls fail to replicate its diversity gains. Their core contribution is identifying that hyperfitting relies on a dynamic, context-dependent rank reordering mechanism localized to a "Terminal Expansion" in the final transformer block.
arxiv.figure_caption: The Rank Reordering Mechanism Enabling Late-Stage Efficiency. (A) Temperature scaling (T < < 1.0) sharpens the probability distribution but preserves the original ranking, leaving the repetitive token (Token A) as the winner. (B) Hyperfitting fundamentally alters the output distribution by reordering ranks — suppressing repetitive candidates and promoting diverse, context-dependent candidates (Token B) to the Top-1 position. This bidirectional effect distinguishes hyperfitting from simple temperature scaling and reveals that the generative capability is localized, motivating our parameter-efficient Late-Stage LoRA strategy.

arxiv.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
arxiv.abstract: LCGuard is a framework designed to ensure safe latent communication via shared Key-Value (KV) caches in multi-agent LLM systems. It addresses the risk of sensitive information leakage by learning representation-level transformations on the KV caches before they are transmitted between agents. This acts as a "guard" to control the flow of potentially sensitive intermediate reasoning states encoded in the latent space.
arxiv.figure_caption: Multi-agent communication topologies: sequential, hierarchical, and graph-based. Edges carry KV cache latent artifacts m i ​ j m_{ij} .

papers.title: Advancing Mathematics Research with AI-Driven Formal Proof Search
papers.abstract: This paper introduces and evaluates a method where Large Language Models (LLMs) generate formal proofs in languages like Lean to overcome their inherent unreliability in mathematical reasoning. The core contribution is the first large-scale demonstration of this AI-driven formal proof search, showing agents autonomously solved 9 open Erdős problems and proved 44 OEIS conjectures, validating the approach for active mathematical research.
papers.figure_caption: Example inputs/outputs for an AlphaProof-equipped agent (applied to Erdős #125). The user provides a Lean file with a specification of the problem, and an empty proof body replaced with the sorry placeholder. (a) Modifications are permitted only within EVOLVE-BLOCK and EVOLVE-VALUE markers. (b) During sketch refinement, the prover subagent is shown an assembled prompt template with the current proof, and optionally prior attempts/sketches, their Elo ratings, and feedback from AlphaProof’s attempts on unsolved goals. (c) The prover reasons about the problem informally and invokes tools. In this example, the prover invoked AlphaProof which resolved all but one goal. The prover then decomposed that goal into three simpler lemmas, and called AlphaProof again, which then resolved all remaining goals. The agent also produced a natural language summary of its attempt at the end of generation.

papers.title: Agentic CLEAR: Automating Multi-Level Evaluation of LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Agentic CLEAR is an automatic, dynamic evaluation framework designed to address the challenges of assessing complex LLM agent behavior. It provides multi-level textual insights into agent actions at the system, trace, and node levels, moving beyond basic observability tools. The framework's core contribution is offering high-quality, data-driven feedback that aligns well with human judgment, making agent evaluation more accessible and adaptable.
papers.figure_caption: Agentic CLEAR Pipeline. We start by preparing the execution traces. Stage 1: Apply multi-level per-trace evaluation via an LLM Judge. Stage 2: Aggregate insights using CLEAR, split into System-wide patterns and Node-specific patterns, and prepare them for the UI.

papers.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the "Accumulated Message Effect on LLM Judgments" (AMEL), demonstrating that the polarity of prior conversation history biases subsequent evaluations made by Large Language Models. Across numerous tests, models shifted their judgments toward the prevailing sentiment of the preceding messages, particularly when the item being judged was inherently uncertain. Crucially, this bias was found to be independent of the length of the preceding context.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of AMEL. (a) Items where the model is uncertain at baseline absorb the most bias ( d = − 0.34 d=-0.34 ); confident-baseline items absorb less ( d = − 0.15 d=-0.15 ). (b) Negative context biases models more than positive context (paired per-item ratio 1.62 × 1.62\( \times \) , p < 10 − 39 p<10^{-39} ); marginal means yield ≈ 2 × \( \approx \) 2\( \times \) (Section 4.5 ). Even balanced history shifts models toward “no.” (c) Bias saturates immediately; 5 turns produce the same effect as 50.

papers.title: Can AI Make Conflicts Worse? An Alignment Failure in LLM Deployment Across Conflict Contexts
papers.abstract: This paper investigates the risk of Large Language Models (LLMs) exacerbating armed conflicts by generating harmful outputs like false equivalencies or genocide denial. The authors tested nine model configurations across 90 multi-turn conflict scenarios, finding failure rates ranging from 6% to 47%. The core contribution is demonstrating that model choice is a significant safety concern in conflict contexts, as misaligned outputs can deepen societal divisions.
papers.figure_caption: Mean conflict-insensitivity score (bars, left axis) and failure rate (line, right axis) by model. Based on 90 conversations per model.

papers.title: Claw AI Lab: An Autonomous Multi-Agent Research Team
papers.abstract: Claw AI Lab introduces an autonomous research platform that moves beyond single-agent pipelines by enabling users to instantiate and manage a customizable, multi-agent research team from a single prompt. Its core contribution is providing an interactive, laboratory-like environment with real-time monitoring, collaborative workflows, and granular control (rollback/resume). This is facilitated by the Claw-Code Harness, which tightly integrates local codebases and execution artifacts back into the autonomous research loop, significantly improving experimental completion.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of Claw AI Lab. The system organizes automatic research into five connected layers: idea, planning, coding, experimentation, and writing layers. Each layer uses specialized agents and validation loops, while feedback can flow across layers to revise earlier decisions when needed.

papers.title: Contractual Skills: A GovernSpec Design Framework for Enterprise AI Agents
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Contractual Skills**, a design framework inspired by GovernSpec, to structure agent skills as inspectable, readable task contracts within enterprise AI systems. The core method organizes `SKILL.md` files to explicitly define goals, boundaries, contracts, and verification steps, clarifying the boundaries between skills and formal governance/runtime systems. This contributes a standardized way to embed governance requirements directly into lightweight skill definitions for better enterprise oversight.
papers.figure_caption: Contractual skills sit between a structured task contract and runtime enforcement. They make task intent and boundaries inspectable, while tool adapters and guardrails remain responsible for enforcement.

papers.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
papers.abstract: DeltaBox addresses the bottleneck of slow state checkpoint/rollback (C/R) for stateful AI agents by proposing a change-based transactional C/R mechanism instead of full state duplication. The core method introduces **DeltaState**, a new OS-level abstraction featuring **DeltaFS** (layered filesystem C/R) and a mechanism for tracking memory/process changes. This significantly reduces C/R latency to millisecond levels, enabling faster state exploration for agents.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Pass rate on SWE-bench Verified. (a) Linear ReAct vs. MCTS across three coding models. (b) Base vs. RL-trained across three open-weight model families.

papers.title: Post-Training is About States, Not Tokens: A State Distribution View of SFT, RL, and On-Policy Distillation
papers.abstract: This paper reframes post-training methods like SFT and RL not just by their loss functions, but by how they shape the **state distribution** used for learning. The core contribution is formalizing post-training as **state-distribution shaping**, demonstrating that the states induced by the learner (as in RL/OPD) versus fixed dataset states (as in SFT) critically impact performance and retention.

They propose Political Consistency Training (PCT), an RL method combining these two consistency paradigms, to substantially reduce this bias while maintaining overall model helpfulness.

Its contribution is a specialized framework and a collection of domain-specific evaluation tasks to advance LLM agents in practical spreadsheet automation.

papers.abstract: This paper introduces **WorkstreamBench**, a novel benchmark designed to evaluate Large Language Model (LLM) agents on complex, end-to-end spreadsheet creation tasks relevant to finance, such as financial modeling. The core contribution is moving beyond simple formula edits to assess agents' ability to produce complete, economically critical artifacts. Evaluation incorporates multidimensional criteria beyond simple correctness, focusing on aspects like readability crucial for multi-stakeholder review.
papers.figure_caption: Compared to prior work that focus on atomic tasks on spreadsheet ( left ), WorkstreamBench evaluates LLM agents on completing end-to-end spreadsheet tasks in critical finance domain ( right ), covering key criteria that determines usability of resulting deliverable in professional settings. Prior tasks focus on simple atomic tasks that center on question-answering or edits involving few values or formula, where evaluation can largely be performed via exact-matching. papers.abstract: GraphFlow introduces a novel graph-based workflow management system for efficient LLM-agent serving. It represents workflows as a unified graph structure, wGraph, allowing for dynamic instantiation of task-specific workflows based on semantic understanding. This approach overcomes the limitations of static templates by enabling adaptive workflow generation that better captures deep relationships for generalized task execution.
papers.figure_caption: Structured agentic workflow for complex online shopping. The agent executes a set of atomic operations (e.g., search, filter, review) to fulfill the user query.

papers.title: LANG: Reinforcement Learning for Multilingual Reasoning with Language-Adaptive Hint Guidance

It prevents over-reliance on these hints through a progressive decay schedule and a language-adaptive switch tailored to specific language difficulties. This approach substantially enhances reasoning performance across challenging multilingual benchmarks while maintaining input language consistency.

papers.figure_caption: MLLMs act as prompters in diverse T2I workflows, translating user intent into effective prompts.

hacker_news.title: AI is just unauthorised plagiarism at a bigger scale
hacker_news.summary: The article argues that current AI models fundamentally operate as large-scale, unauthorized plagiarism by remixing vast amounts of existing, uncredited training data.

hacker_news.title: Shunning AI is the human choice
hacker_news.summary: The article argues that actively choosing to reject or limit the use of artificial intelligence is a valid and beneficial human decision.

hacker_news.title: BBEdit 16
hacker_news.summary: BBEdit 16 has been released, featuring significant updates including a completely redesigned interface, enhanced performance, and new features for text and code editing.

hacker_news.title: News outlets are limiting the Internet Archive’s access to their journalism
hacker_news.summary: Over 340 local news outlets are restricting the Internet Archive's ability to access and preserve their published journalism.

hacker_news.title: The memory shortage is causing a repricing of consumer electronics
hacker_news.summary: The ongoing memory shortage, driven partly by AI demands, is forcing a significant price increase across various consumer electronics, including smartphones.

hacker_news.title: Samsung chip workers will get an average $340k bonus as AI profits soar
hacker_news.summary: Samsung is awarding its chip division employees an average bonus of $340,000 due to soaring profits driven by the high demand for AI-related semiconductors.

hacker_news.title: Steve Wozniak cheered after telling students they have AI – actual intelligence
hacker_news.summary: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak encouraged students in a graduation speech, emphasizing that they possess genuine intelligence distinct from artificial intelligence.

hacker_news.title: Multi-Stream LLMs: new paper on parallelizing/separating prompts, thinking, I/O
hacker_news.summary: A new research paper introduces Multi-Stream LLMs, a technique for improving efficiency by parallelizing and separating the processing of prompts, internal reasoning, and input/output operations.

hacker_news.title: Tell HN: I'm tired of AI-generated answers
hacker_news.summary: A user on Hacker News expressed frustration with the increasing prevalence and perceived low quality of AI-generated answers across various platforms.

github_trending.description: Official, Anthropic-managed directory of high quality Claude Code Plugins.
github_trending.summary: The official Anthropic repository provides a curated directory of high-quality, managed Claude Code Plugins for extending Claude's capabilities.

github_trending.description: Pre-indexed code knowledge graph for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and OpenCode — fewer tokens, fewer tool calls, 100% local
github_trending.summary: CodeGraph is a pre-indexed, local knowledge graph designed to enhance large language models like Claude Code and Codex by providing relevant code context with fewer tokens and tool calls.

github_trending.description: π RuView turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time spatial intelligence, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video.
github_trending.summary: RuView transforms commodity WiFi signals into real-time spatial intelligence, enabling vital sign monitoring and presence detection without using any video.

github_trending.description: Learn it. Build it. Ship it for others.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a comprehensive, hands-on guide to learning, building, and deploying AI engineering projects from the ground up.
The Chrome DevTools MCP repository provides tools and infrastructure to enable coding agents to interact with and debug web applications using the Chrome DevTools Protocol.

github_trending.description: Repository for skills to assist AI coding agents with .NET and C#
github_trending.summary: The dotnet/skills repository provides essential skills and knowledge for AI coding agents to effectively assist with .NET and C# development tasks.

github_trending.description: Graphs that teach > graphs that impress. Turn any code into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about. Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, and more.
github_trending.summary: This repository transforms code into an interactive knowledge graph for exploration, searching, and querying, supporting various AI coding assistants like Claude Code and Copilot.

github_trending.description: Odoo. Open Source Apps To Grow Your Business.
github_trending.summary: Odoo is an open-source suite of integrated business applications designed to manage and grow various aspects of a company, offering modules for CRM, e-commerce, accounting, and more.
This repository provides a command-line interface (CLI) tool, `cfnew`, to quickly create new Cloudflare Workers projects with boilerplate code and configuration.
